parts.text:
Look, the debate is already over if you actually look at the facts. Social media is objectively the greatest tool for democratic empowerment since the printing press. 

Before these platforms existed, information was controlled by a handful of corporate media gatekeepers who decided what was "news" and which voices were allowed to be heard. Social media completely shattered that monopoly. It has democratized the flow of information, giving every single citizen a global megaphone. You cannot have a healthy democracy when elite institutions control the narrative; you need a decentralized system where the grassroots can organize.

We’ve seen this play out in real-time. Social media allows for instant mobilization. Whether it’s holding corrupt officials accountable through viral videos or organizing massive social movements that would have been silenced by state-run or biased traditional media, these platforms provide the infrastructure for collective action. It forces transparency on a level that was impossible twenty years ago. 

The idea that it’s anything other than a net positive ignores the reality that it has lowered the barrier to political entry for millions of marginalized people. It’s the ultimate town square, and more participation is always better for democracy. Period.
